title: Iterators and invalidation deterministic evidence bank

items.title: Iterators and invalidation: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? The end iterator refers to the last element and can be dereferenced before a loop stops.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Standard ranges are half-open: end is one past the final element and must never be dereferenced.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Standard ranges are half-open: end is one past the final element and must never be dereferenced.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Standard ranges are half-open: end is one past the final element and must never be dereferenced.
The end iterator refers to the last element and can be dereferenced before a loop stops.

answer.explanation: Standard ranges are half-open: end is one past the final element and must never be dereferenced.

items.title: Iterators and invalidation: executable application
items.prompt: Read a vector and use iterators to sum the half-open interior range excluding the first and last elements; print zero when fewer than three elements exist.
